puzzle.title: The Towering Challenge
puzzle.intro: Before a decisive battle, a formidable figure steps forward, their immense presence casting a shadow of doubt over the opposing army. Confident in their own might, this individual issues a challenge for single combat, believing none would dare to face them. This moment would become a testament to the nature of true strength, proving that worldly power is nothing before the will of Allah.
clues: I was a champion of my people, known for my immense physical size and strength.
My army and I advanced against a small band of believers led by their king, Talut.
Full of arrogance, I strode out between the armies and demanded a duel to decide the battle's outcome.
I looked upon my opponents with contempt, certain that my power was unmatchable.
A young shepherd boy, armed with only a sling, bravely stepped forward to accept my challenge.
Despite my imposing stature and armor, I was defeated by a single projectile from this young challenger, who would later become a great prophet and king.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Jalut (Goliath)
Namrud
Fir'awn (Pharaoh)
The Samiri
Tubba'

trial.label: What Was My Challenge?
options: Single combat against a young believer
Building a tower to reach the heavens
Demanding prostration from a prophet
Fashioning a golden calf for worship
Debating with Prophet Ibrahim about Allah
trial.answer: Single combat against a young believer

location.label: What Was My Downfall?
options: A stone from a sling
A devastating flood
A swarm of birds with baked-clay stones
Being swallowed by the earth
Drowning in a parted sea

outcome.label: What Was the Result of My Defeat?
options: The victory of Talut's army and the rise of Dawud
My entire army being drowned in pursuit of a prophet
The establishment of the Kaaba as a house of worship
My people being punished with a mighty shriek
My kingdom being inherited by Prophet Sulayman
outcome.answer: The victory of Talut's army and the rise of Dawud

puzzle.arabic: فَهَزَمُوهُم بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ وَقَتَلَ دَاوُۥدُ جَالُوتَ وَءَاتَىٰهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمُلْكَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَهُۥ مِمَّا يَشَآءُ ۗ وَلَوْلَا دَفْعُ ٱللَّهِ ٱلنَّاسَ بَعْضَهُم بِبَعْضٍۢ لَّفَسَدَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ
puzzle.verse: So they defeated them by permission of Allāh, and David killed Goliath, and Allāh gave him the kingship and wisdom [i.e., prophethood] and taught him from that which He willed. And if it were not for Allāh checking [some] people by means of others, the earth would have been corrupted, but Allāh is the possessor of bounty for the worlds. (2:251)
